questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Number of aromatic compounds among the following:
questions.solution:
Aromatic compounds
A layer of graphite (graphene) contains infinite lattice of fused aromatic rings. All the valencies are satisfied (except at the edges) and no bonds are needed between layers. (Planar layered structure of fused aromatic rings).

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The compound ' X ' is composed of carbon, Hydrogen and Oxygen. It has 2 geometrical isomers. It has 4 stereoisomers. All 4 stereoisomers are optically active. If X is the smallest alkenoic acid which satisfies all these conditions then calculate total number of carbon present in compound X.
questions.solution:
The smallest optically active alkenoic acid which can also show geometrical isomerism can be written as
Hence its molecular formula is $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}_2$.

questions.q:
For how many 2-substituted ethanols, $\mathrm{GCH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}$, the gauche conformation is more stable than the anti:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { anti: } \\
& \mathrm{G}=-\mathrm{OH},-\mathrm{NH}_2,-\mathrm{F},-\mathrm{Cl},-\mathrm{OCH}_3,-\mathrm{NHCH}_3,-\mathrm{N}\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3,-\mathrm{NO}_2 .
\end{aligned}$
questions.solution: Except - Cl all can undergo intramolecular H -bonding.
